twitter_thread: 47 out of 54 AirDrop vulnerabilities in 2022 were caused by protocol prying, exposing 250,000 devices.
Wireshark and tcpdump can intercept AirDrop traffic, revealing sensitive data like phone numbers and emails.
A misconfigured AirDrop setting at a university exposed 5,000 student devices to protocol prying attacks.
MDNS and TCP/IP used in AirDrop allow attackers to intercept files and inject malware.
Implementing end-to-end encryption, like TLS 1.3, can reduce AirDrop vulnerabilities by 60%.
Prioritizing protocol security can prevent 3 out of 4 AirDrop and Quick Share vulnerabilities.
What's the most effective protocol security measure you've implemented in your own projects? #airdrop #protocolprying
pack.linkedin: In 2022, 47 out of 54 AirDrop vulnerabilities were caused by protocol prying, affecting 250,000 devices. The use of MDNS and TCP/IP in AirDrop makes it vulnerable to protocol prying attacks. Implementing end-to-end encryption, like TLS 1.3, can reduce vulnerabilities by 60%. What specific measures have you taken to secure protocol communication in your work?
tiktok_hooks: I used Wireshark to intercept my own AirDrop traffic and you won't believe what I found
Your phone's file sharing feature is hiding a dangerous secret

pack.reddit_headline: Can Protocol Prying Break AirDrop and Quick Share?
